The musician "listens to the produced attempt, notices differences between the sound of the desired goal and the current attempt, and makes the difference the target for a new attempt." No external grader — the *desired-goal representation* is the comparator, and the perceived discrepancy is the reward. The Army AAR is the same move at team scale: "the gap between actual and intended cannot be examined without a shared reference" — but that reference is the team's OWN prior intent, self-set, not a benchmark. This is how "better-than" is earned without an oracle: A>B because A's outcome landed CLOSER to a self-articulated prediction/goal than B's did.

2. SURPRISE AS THE TRIGGER (Schön). "Better-than" reasoning only fires when a case violates knowing-in-action: "an element of surprise prompts reflection-in-action... the practitioner surfaces and criticizes their initial understanding, constructs a new description, and TESTS the new description by an on-the-spot experiment." The comparison is expectation-vs-surprise; the "experiment" is a within-episode A/B where the practitioner is their own judge. Better-than is inferred from *which reframing dissolved the surprise*.

3. CONTRAST ACROSS REPEATED ATTEMPTS (deliberate practice = "well-defined task + immediate feedback + repetition + exploit errors"). Better-than emerges from RANKING many attempts against the same self-set goal — the comparison population is your own past attempts, not other people. This is the "10,000 hours" substance: not clock time but thousands of goal-referenced discrepancy-corrections.

4. CASE-BASED RETAIN/REVISE. CBR earns better-than structurally: retrieve similar past case → reuse its solution → "evaluate the result; if the outcome is unsatisfactory, the solution is REFINED" → retain. "Learning is a by-product of every problem-solving episode." The revised case out-ranks the prior case for future retrieval *because* the prior one's outcome was observed unsatisfactory against the goal — an oracle-free re-ranking driven by observed-outcome-vs-intent, exactly the kit's used-vs-ignored / recall-outcome loop.
lenses.what_it_requires:
Four preconditions, and a single-user session meets THREE of them but structurally strains the fourth:

- A SELF-ARTICULATED GOAL/EXPECTATION *before* acting (the comparator). MET in cmk if the agent records an intent/prediction before a method — this is why cmk's `/goal` and a lightweight "expected outcome" note are the highest-leverage additions: without a pre-registered expectation there is no oracle-free reference to measure against. AAR is explicit: intent must be surfaced FIRST or the gap is unexaminable.
- REPETITION / RECURRENCE of comparable episodes (contrast needs ≥2 attempts at the "same" task). PARTIAL. A single user hits recurring task-shapes (same test cmd, same file, same class of bug) but rarely enough clean repeats of one decision for statistical confidence — the kit already tracks recurrence_count, which is the right primitive, but N is small.
- FEEDBACK that is TIMELY and DIAGNOSTIC (not just pass/fail — it must say *how far off*). PARTIAL-to-MET: tool exit-codes, contradiction, recall-miss are timely; but they are coarse (binary), where deliberate practice needs graded discrepancy. The kit's honest feedback is mostly failure-shaped (prune signals), matching the settled asymmetry.
- A stable-enough WORLD that the goal stays valid across repeats. MET within a project, THREATENED by codebase drift (a method that was better last month may not be now — the reference itself expires).

A single-user bounded session CAN run the loop for within-session reflection-on-action (surprise → reframe → retain the reframing), and across-session via the recurrence counter — but it CANNOT reach Ericsson-grade earned judgment, which explicitly requires *years* of goal-referenced repetition with a coach shaping the goal. The kit has no coach and few repeats.
lenses.failure_modes:
The self-generated reference is exactly what makes this oracle-free AND exactly what makes it corruptible — the reference can be WRONG and then SELF-CONFIRM. Adversarially:

- SUPERSTITIOUS LEARNING / spurious credit assignment. When the goal is met, the learner credits whatever they happened to do — including irrelevant ritual. One success → "this method is better" is the single most dangerous inference (the maintainer's "overfitting to one success" named directly). Human experts avoid it only via *many* repeats; with N=1-3 the kit is squarely in the superstition zone. Prune-on-failure survives this (a failed method is honestly worse); reinforce-on-success does NOT (a lucky success falsely ranks A>B). This is independent corroboration of the kit's settled asymmetry from the human side.
- ILLUSION OF COMPETENCE / self-confirming reference. If the agent both SETS the expectation and JUDGES the outcome, it can lower the bar to match what happened ("that's basically what I predicted"). Dunning-Kruger: the weakest lack the skill to see their own gap. A self-graded prediction-error is only as honest as the prediction's precision — a vague goal always looks "met."
- CONFOUNDING: the two methods were never applied to the SAME situation (different files, different day, different context), so the outcome gap reflects the situation, not the method. AAR guards this with "documenting reality descriptively, not attributively"; the kit has no such guard by default.
- DELAYED/ABSENT feedback → the discrepancy signal never arrives, so no learning (or worse, the wrong episode gets credited). Success is silent (settled) — so the reference-vs-outcome loop is structurally starved of positive discrepancies and over-fed negative ones.
- REFERENCE EXPIRY / Goodhart: once "prefer method A" is stored, the agent optimizes to the stored judgment rather than re-testing — freezing a once-true better-than past its validity as the codebase drifts.
lenses.transferable_kernel: PRE-REGISTER THE EXPECTATION, THEN STORE THE PREDICTION-ERROR, NOT THE VERDICT. The one transferable idea: before the agent commits to a method/approach, have it record a one-line EXPECTED outcome ("this should make the test pass / this refactor should cut the file below N lines / I expect the user to accept this edit"). Next turn, the automatic oracle-free signals cmk ALREADY has (tool exit-code, user-correction, used-vs-ignored, recall-miss, contradiction) resolve that prediction as hit/miss — and the memory the kit writes is the *discrepancy*: "predicted X, observed Y, method was A." This converts a bare fact into an earned judgment WITHOUT any oracle or human grade, because the reference was self-set and the observation is automatic. Three guardrails carried over verbatim from the human failure modes: (1) require a pre-action expectation or DON'T claim better-than — no reference, no judgment (the AAR "intent first" rule); (2) never promote A>B from a single success — gate comparative claims behind recurrence_count ≥ k with a CONSISTENT direction, since one success is superstition (the deliberate-practice repeat requirement); (3) keep the asymmetry — a violated prediction (predicted-pass, got-fail) is trustworthy and should down-rank the method; a met prediction is weak evidence and should at most nudge, never lock, because self-graded success self-confirms. Net: the kit can move from FACTS to earned JUDGMENTS oracle-free ONLY for the prune direction and ONLY when an expectation was pre-registered and repeated — which is real, honest, single-user-achievable learning, but is narrower than "the system knows A is better" and must be labeled as such.

lenses.lens: Multi-armed bandits / online experimentation (BAI, Thompson sampling, contextual/dueling bandits, non-stationary UCB, off-policy evaluation)
lenses.how_better_than_is_inferred_without_orac: Bandits earn "arm A > arm B" WITHOUT an answer key by a single mechanism: repeatedly PULL each arm, keep a running empirical mean plus a CONFIDENCE INTERVAL around it, and declare A>B only once A's lower confidence bound provably exceeds B's upper bound. No gold label is ever consulted — the ranking is a statistical consequence of accumulated noisy outcomes. Pure-exploration/best-arm-identification (successive elimination, LUCB, racing, SPRT) makes this the explicit goal: sample the contested arms, shrink the intervals, eliminate an arm the moment its interval separates from the leader's. Thompson sampling does the soft version — maintain a posterior per arm, sample from it, and the better arm naturally gets pulled more as evidence accrues (exploration decays as confidence grows). Three refinements matter for oracle-free memory: (1) DUELING BANDITS drop the absolute reward entirely — you never score A or B, you only observe "A beat B this round," and build a preference matrix p_ij, then define the winner relationally (Condorcet: beats all others; Copeland/Borda: best win-rate). This is the closest analog to a memory kit, which never gets a score, only "the approach recalled here was/ wasn't followed by trouble." (2) NON-STATIONARY bandits (sliding-window UCB, discounted UCB, CUSUM change-detection) earn "A>B *right now*" by down-weighting or forgetting old pulls — the ranking is always over a recent window, because the environment drifts. (3) OFF-POLICY EVALUATION is the only sub-lens that judges an arm you did NOT pull, via importance-weighting logged outcomes by action probabilities — but it needs both the logged reward AND the logging probabilities, so it is not truly oracle-free.
lenses.what_it_requires: Hard preconditions, and a single-user bounded session meets almost none of them. (1) REPEATED PULLS of the SAME arm under comparable conditions — the whole apparatus is a law-of-large-numbers machine. (2) A per-pull REWARD SIGNAL, even noisy/delayed/binary — Thompson/UCB need a scalar, dueling needs a binary win/loss, OPE needs a logged reward. (3) BOTH arms actually TRIED (or, for OPE, non-zero logging probability on the un-tried arm, else importance weights explode). (4) Enough pulls to overcome the sample-complexity floor: fixed-confidence BAI needs on the order of Σ Δ⁻²·ln(1/δ) pulls — INVERSE-SQUARED in the gap Δ between arms. A modest gap (0.1) at modest confidence (95%) already demands hundreds of pulls PER ARM; a subtle "A is slightly better" needs thousands. (5) For non-stationary methods, enough pulls WITHIN each stationary window. A single user in a bounded session gives you: a handful of episodes (not thousands), each context near-unique (few true repeats of "the same arm"), reward that is usually absent and when present is delayed by many turns or to a later session, and drift fast enough that any window large enough to be statistically meaningful already spans a preference change. The one precondition a session CAN meet is a coarse binary/relational outcome per episode (failure-signal fired or not) — which points straight at the dueling reformulation, not classic reward-bandits.
lenses.failure_modes: (1) SAMPLE STARVATION is fatal and non-negotiable: with Δ⁻² scaling, a single user never accumulates the pulls to cross the confidence threshold for anything but a huge gap — so any "A>B" the kit declares from a few episodes is statistically indistinguishable from noise; asserting it is CHEATING with a small-sample point estimate dressed as a judgment. (2) NON-STATIONARITY vs sample-count is a vice: the window must be short (preferences drift) yet long (statistics need pulls) — for one user these two constraints have no overlap, so you're always either stale or under-powered. (3) DELAYED/ABSENT REWARD breaks the estimator's identifiability: the delayed-dueling result shows you literally cannot distinguish "outcome not yet observed" from "arm lost," biasing the loser; unbounded delay forces an arbitrary M-threshold "presume it never happened," which silently mislabels slow-payoff approaches as failures — exactly the kit's condition where a good recall's benefit is invisible/late. (4) SELF-CONFIRMATION / feedback loop: once the kit prefers A it recalls A more, so A gets more "pulls" and B starves — the classic bandit collapse-to-a-suboptimal-arm, here with no exploration budget to escape it (Thompson's exploration needs the reward signal the kit lacks). (5) GOODHART on the proxy: substituting "fewer failure-signals fired" for true reward means the kit optimizes signal-suppression, not quality (an approach that avoids checkable outputs fires fewer failures). (6) OPE's importance weights EXPLODE for the rarely-tried alternative — the un-deployed approach is precisely the one with tiny logging probability, so its counterfactual estimate is the noisiest, defeating the point.
lenses.transferable_kernel: Reframe memory-learning as a DELAYED-FEEDBACK DUELING bandit over a SLIDING WINDOW, not a reward bandit — and inherit the honesty, not the promise. Concretely: (a) NEVER try to score an approach absolutely (the kit has no reward oracle); instead accumulate only RELATIVE outcomes — "when approach A was recalled, a failure-signal followed less often than when B was" — a per-pair win-count, exactly the dueling p_ij matrix, buildable from the automatic prune-on-failure signals the kit already has (tool-result, user-correction, contradiction, cmk-forget). (b) Apply a CONFIDENCE GATE as a first-class output: only promote "A>B" to a durable JUDGMENT when the relational win-count crosses a threshold; below it, store it as a PROVISIONAL hypothesis explicitly tagged low-confidence (n=2 is a hunch, not a finding) — this makes the sample-complexity floor a feature (the kit tells the user "I've only seen this twice") rather than a hidden lie. (c) Adopt SLIDING-WINDOW / discount expiry directly: a comparative judgment carries a recency window and DECAYS — an "A>B" earned six months and one project-pivot ago is stale, mirroring discounted-UCB; pair with cheap CHANGE-DETECTION (a user correction that reverses a prior duel = a change-point that resets the window). (d) Borrow the delayed-dueling M-threshold as an explicit, tunable "benefit-observation horizon" so slow-payoff approaches aren't auto-labeled losers — and log the identifiability limit honestly. The HONEST BREAK to state plainly: classic reward-bandits/Thompson/BAI need scale (Δ⁻² pulls) and a scalar reward a single user cannot supply — do NOT import them or their regret guarantees. What survives is the SHAPE — relative-not-absolute comparison, an explicit confidence gate that gates promotion, and windowed decay — which composes cleanly with the kit's already-settled prune-on-failure asymmetry and turns "we tried X then Y, Y worked better" into a provisional, decaying, sample-count-annotated judgment instead of a fabricated certainty.

lenses.lens: scientific-method / epistemics — inference-to-the-better-explanation via single-subject (N-of-1) causal inference
lenses.how_better_than_is_inferred_without_orac: Science earns "A>B" without a ground-truth answer key by CONTROLLED COMPARISON plus REPLICATION, not by scoring against a key. The exact sub-field for a one-subject, no-oracle setting is N-of-1 / single-subject causal inference, whose whole purpose is to conclude "A is better than B FOR THIS ONE SUBJECT" from repeated within-subject observation. The mechanics: (1) Be-your-own-control / within-subject blocking — compare A-periods against B-periods for the SAME subject, which differences out that subject's stable traits (the largest confounder in a cross-subject study is gone for free). (2) Replication across multiple A/B periods — "A worked once" is worthless (the single-success trap + regression-to-the-mean); "A out-performed B across k alternations" is evidence, with variance ≈ σ²/(t·α(1-α)) so precision grows only with more periods t. (3) Ablation / "all-else-equal" attribution — change ONE thing, hold the rest constant; a difference in effect then licenses "that factor is a cause." (4) Reversal (A/B/A) — the strongest oracle-free signal: subject tries A, reverts to B, the effect reverses with the treatment. (5) Anytime-valid inference / e-values — the correct math to accumulate evidence episode-by-episode and legitimately "peek"/stop at any time WITHOUT inflating false positives (classical p-values break under this; e-processes don't). The CRITICAL, honesty-forcing distinction: N1RT (randomized self-experiment — the subject deliberately randomizes which method they use) earns a genuine causal claim; N1OS (OBSERVATIONAL — the subject uses whatever method they feel like) is the weakest form and the literature explicitly scopes it to "discovering causal effects and possible treatments for SUBSEQUENT TESTING." Better-than in the observational case is inferred as a provisional hypothesis, not confirmed causation.
lenses.what_it_requires: Preconditions, and whether a single-user session can meet them: (1) REPEATED TRIALS of both arms — you need multiple A-periods and multiple B-periods over time; a single session almost never has this (met only ACROSS sessions, which is exactly the cross-session substrate a memory kit is). (2) BOTH ARMS OBSERVED under comparable conditions — met weakly: a real user rarely runs A and B on the same task; usually you get A on task-1, B on task-2, so the comparison is confounded by task. (3) NO comparison population needed — this is the win: N-of-1 replaces the between-subjects population with WITHIN-subject time periods, so a single user IS a valid unit of analysis (the "person as population" framing). (4) TIME / enough periods — precision requires many periods; a single-user kit accumulates them slowly, so any judgment is low-n for a long time. (5) STATIONARITY — the subject/context must not drift between the A-periods and the B-periods; a single-user dev project drifts constantly (new tooling, changed codebase, learning), which a kit CANNOT assume away. Bottom line: a single-user session meets the "no population needed / person-as-population" precondition natively, but structurally FAILS the randomization, comparable-conditions, and stationarity preconditions — so it operates in the N1OS (observational, weakest) regime, capable of ranked hypotheses, not confirmed causal claims.
lenses.failure_modes: The precise ways an oracle-free "A>B" goes wrong, each named in the epistemics literature and each one a passive memory kit walks straight into: (1) CONFOUNDING — an unmeasured cause of BOTH the choice-of-method AND the outcome (the user picked A on the EASY tasks; A "wins" for a reason that isn't A). Fatal and unfixable in purely observational data. (2) NON-STATIONARITY / time-trend — A was tried later, after the user got better / the tooling improved; A "wins" only because time moved. (3) CARRYOVER / no-washout — the previous method's effect bleeds into the current period, so you can't attribute the outcome to the current method. (4) SINGLE-SUCCESS TRAP + REGRESSION TO THE MEAN — promoting A off one good outcome; extreme results naturally revert, and the reversion gets miscredited to whatever you did next. (5) GARDEN OF FORKING PATHS — choosing WHICH A-vs-B comparison to record AFTER seeing which one looked good manufactures false better-thans even with zero conscious fishing (Gelman/Loken: no p-hacking required). (6) P-VALUE PEEKING / OPTIONAL STOPPING — a kit that re-ranks every session and promotes A the moment cumulative evidence first crosses a threshold inflates false positives (the exact thing anytime-valid/e-value methods exist to fix). (7) FUNDAMENTAL PROBLEM OF CAUSAL INFERENCE — you never observe both counterfactuals for the same turn, so every single-turn "A would have been better" is unfalsifiable. (8) SELF-CONFIRMATION — the kit surfaces A, the user uses A because it was surfaced, the outcome is fine, the kit reads that as "A confirmed" — a feedback loop where the memory manufactures its own supporting evidence (the recall-then-comply-then-count-it-as-success trap).
lenses.transferable_kernel: The kernel: model earned method-judgments as N1OS-GRADE RANKED HYPOTHESES, not facts — and let the epistemics cap the CLAIM STRENGTH honestly rather than fake an oracle. Concretely for cmk: store a method-judgment as {claim:"A>B", conditions, n_periods, confound_flags, status:"provisional"} and NEVER as a bare "A is better." Four load-bearing moves, each free of any oracle: (a) require n>1 within-subject repetitions before surfacing anything — reuse the recurrence counter you already have, but READ IT AS REPLICATION, not reinforcement (one success = the single-success trap). (b) Attach a confound caveat by construction: if A and B were tried in different project eras/tooling/task-difficulties, flag "time/context-confounded" and DOWN-rank the claim — you cannot remove the confound, but you can refuse to hide it. (c) Prefer REVERSAL evidence (user tried A, reverted to B, stayed on B) as the single strongest oracle-free signal a lone user emits — an A/B/A pattern is the reversal design, and it aligns with the prune>reinforce asymmetry (a revert is a within-subject failure signal; a silent success is unobservable). (d) Accumulate evidence as e-values / anytime-valid, not as a p<threshold crossed once, so re-ranking every session is mathematically legitimate. And surface the result at its true strength: "A has tended to beat B for you under conditions C (n=k, unconfirmed)" — a provisional lead a human can test, which is exactly what N1OS is designed to produce. This does NOT need scale or an oracle; it needs the discipline to stamp the judgment with its n, its confounds, and the word "provisional."

synthesis.what_is_known:
The mechanics of earning "A>B" that the field ACTUALLY HAS, grouped honestly:

(1) THE ONE ROBUST EARNING MECHANISM — RE-RUN-AND-CHECK against an outcome verifier. Every cohort system that earns a CRISP A>B does it by executing candidate methods and scoring the result: SkillRevise re-executes and keeps the first verifier-passing skill (argmax utility, g_succ=1[succ]); SkillAdaptor accepts a revised skill only if Δ=E[M(K+)]−E[M(K)]≥0 on a counterfactual re-run; AlphaOPT scores each Condition refinement by p_i = (kept+corrected+recovered)/|R_i|, every term decided by "did the program hit the ground-truth optimal value"; GEPA's StrictImprovementAcceptance is literally sum(scores_after) > sum(scores_before). This is real, rigorous, and REPRODUCIBLE — but every instance requires a re-runnable task + a checkable outcome.

(2) THE SPLIT — reinforce-on-success vs prune-on-failure is done by BUCKETING trajectories with a success label, then distilling prose. SkillRL preserves T+={r=1}/T−={r=0} and hands the teacher pre-labeled buckets; ReasoningBank writes "validated strategies" from success and "counterfactual pitfalls" from failure; ExpeL's compare-mode pairs one oracle-confirmed success with one oracle-confirmed failure and extracts the do-X-not-Y rule. The LLM only writes prose; the LABEL does the earning.

(3) THE GENUINELY ORACLE-FREE MECHANISMS — three, and they are narrower than they look:
  (a) SELF-CONTRAST OVER REPLAYS (ReasoningBank/MaTTS): generate k trajectories for the SAME query, feed all at once, ask "why did some succeed while others failed" — A>B inferred from OBSERVABLE OUTCOME DIFFERENCES between siblings, no score. Requires same-task replay (a resettable environment).
  (b) SELF-VERIFIED ACCUMULATION (MUSE): an LLM Reflect-Agent answers "did THIS attempt meet its own checklist" (truthfulness/deliverable/data-fidelity) by re-running tools against the live environment — a per-episode PASS/FAIL, not a between-method comparison. Oracle-free precisely because it substitutes the cheaper question and never actually compares A to B.
  (c) CONSISTENCY-AS-TRUTH (from the RLHF/TCR lens, not the cohort): "better-than" earned by fitting a latent strength (Bradley-Terry) to a GRAPH of many overlapping pairwise verdicts, where transitivity denoises — cycles = measurement error, delete the edges that break the most cycles, surviving order is the ranking. Ground truth replaced by internal consistency. Requires scale (n≥4-8 comparisons per item).

(4) THE VOTE/PRUNE SURVIVAL GATE (ExpeL, oracle-free mechanic even though its labels aren't): an insight's importance count increments on UPVOTE/EDIT, decrements on DOWNVOTE, and the insight is REMOVED at count==0. The number gates survival — the counter-example to the field-wide "inert utility field."

(5) THE PROPOSE/DISPOSE SEPARATION (GEPA, SkillAdaptor): the ORACLE-FREE half is the PROPOSAL — reflection over a failure trace writes a candidate better method with no label needed. The oracle-BOUND half is ADJUDICATION — a scalar/re-run confirms it. This separation is the single cleanest transferable structural insight: you can always propose oracle-free; you can rarely adjudicate oracle-free.
synthesis.what_is_unsolved:
The genuinely OPEN part, stated plainly: NOBODY in this cohort earns a CRISP, VERIFIED "method A > method B" oracle-free at single-user scale from passive observation of ONE non-repeatable trajectory. That exact regime — cmk's regime — is unsolved.

Precisely what's missing and why:

(1) NO ONE COMPARES TWO RIVAL METHODS FOR THE SAME GOAL WITHOUT EITHER (a) an answer key or (b) same-task replay. The oracle-free cohort earns something WEAKER than A>B: MUSE earns "this attempt passed its own checklist" (per-episode pass/fail, not comparison — and explicitly lists contrastive success/fail learning as FUTURE work); ReasoningBank's real A>B (MaTTS self-contrast) needs k replays of the SAME query side-by-side, which a real conversation cannot furnish; AWM refuses to rank at all (random_group_sample keeps one success at random). The comparison layer the maintainer wants is precisely the layer every oracle-free system omits or defers.

(2) THE SINGLE-ARM PROBLEM (the fundamental causal-inference obstacle, confirmed from the bandit + N-of-1 + RLHF lenses independently). A live session runs method A; method B is the road not taken. You never observe both counterfactuals for the same turn — so "B would have been worse" is unfalsifiable, and preference learning's (prompt, chosen, rejected) pair simply doesn't exist. cmk observes ONE arm per episode; earning A>B needs TWO.

(3) THE SCALE FLOOR. Bandit best-arm-identification needs ~Δ⁻²·ln(1/δ) pulls PER ARM (hundreds for a modest gap); Bradley-Terry/TCR need n≥4-8 overlapping comparisons per item; industrial preference sets are 10^4-10^5 pairs. A single user emits maybe DOZENS of durable method-verdicts per YEAR. Any "A>B" declared from 1-3 episodes is a small-sample point estimate dressed as a judgment — statistically indistinguishable from noise.

(4) THE SILENT-SUCCESS ASYMMETRY makes even the reinforce direction structurally starved: a good silent recall leaves NO trace (used it, worked, user moved on), while failure leaves a correction/re-ask/red-test/forget. So the oracle-free signal stream is almost entirely negative — you can prune far more reliably than you can promote, which means "A is better" (a promotion claim) is exactly the direction the kit is WORST equipped to earn.

(5) THE SELF-JUDGE CIRCULARITY CEILING. cmk's only always-available rater is the same model that produced the method. Measured self-preference bias is 10-25% (Claude worse than GPT-4); self-confirmation compounds errors instead of cancelling them (recall-then-comply-then-count-as-success). Without a second independent rater, any model-generated A>B is the model agreeing with itself.

Honest headline: earning verified comparative method-judgment is SOLVED with an oracle (re-run-and-check) and PARTIALLY solved oracle-free WITH replay/scale (self-contrast, consistency-graphs) — but it is UNSOLVED oracle-free at single-user, single-arm, non-replayable, low-n scale. "We don't really know" is the correct maintainer's answer; the evidence supports it.
synthesis.cohort_verdict:
Tally of the 10 skill-memory systems on whether they EARN A>B oracle-free or CHEAT with a benchmark:

ORACLE-BOUND (cheat — earned A>B rides a ground-truth/benchmark answer key; NOT transferable to cmk): SkillRL (binary r(τ) from simulator → GRPO advantage), SkillRevise (benchmark pass/fail verifier + g_succ gate), SkillAdaptor (Δ≥0 re-run against benchmark success indicator), AlphaOPT (every p_i term = "hit the ground-truth optimal value"), ExpeL (gold success label from environment feeds the contrast), Voyager (inspectable world-state oracle — item counts/tech-tree — and it doesn't even earn A>B, only "one program passed"). That's 6 clearly oracle-bound.

ORACLE-DELEGATED / MIXED (swap the answer key for an LLM-judge or a re-runnable observed outcome — oracle-free-ISH but relocates rather than removes the arbiter): AWM (online mode uses Pan-2024 LLM-judge, but REFUSES to rank — keeps one success at random), GEPA (oracle-agnostic architecture but every actual A>B decision consumes a scalar μ that must be re-scorable — gold-answer in headline benchmarks, observed cost/latency otherwise).

GENUINELY ORACLE-FREE at memory-formation (2, both caveated): ReasoningBank (self-contrast IS oracle-free — but its STRONG version needs same-task replay cmk lacks; its transferable base path is a noisy LLM-as-judge binary label, 72.7% raw agreement). MUSE (self-verified accumulation, no benchmark at learning time, closest-to-cmk in shape — BUT it is oracle-free precisely BECAUSE it never earns A>B; it substitutes per-episode pass/fail and defers contrastive learning to future work).

VERDICT: The cohort does NOT earn oracle-free comparative method-judgment. Of 10, six cheat with a benchmark, two delegate to an LLM-judge and/or refuse to rank, and the two genuinely oracle-free systems either need replay (ReasoningBank-strong) or don't actually do the comparison (MUSE). ZERO systems earn a verified A>B oracle-free from a single non-repeatable trajectory. The maintainer's suspicion is correct: the earned-comparative-judgment result in this literature is overwhelmingly oracle-bound, and where it isn't, it's because the system quietly answered an easier question.
synthesis.memory_shape_for_judgments:
The best markdown-native shape for an EARNED method-judgment (vs a plain fact). A plain cmk fact is a flat assertion; a judgment must carry its comparison, its baseline, its evidence-count, its confounds, and its confidence — so it can be REVISED and so it never lies about how much it knows. Concrete file: `context/memory/judgment_<slug>.md`

```markdown
---
type: judgment
id: L-XXXXXXXX
claim: "for <task-shape T>, method A preferred over method B"
baseline: B                      # the fixed reference — NEVER an absolute "A is good"
status: provisional              # provisional | corroborated | contested | retracted
n_episodes: 3                    # replication count (READ AS REPLICATION, not reinforcement)
direction_consistent: true       # did every episode point the same way?
confounds: [time-drift, different-files]   # A and B tried in different eras/tasks → flagged, not hidden
outcome_horizon: short           # when the benefit/failure is observable (delayed-dueling M)
last_seen: 2026-07-01
decays_after: 2026-10-01         # a judgment EXPIRES; codebase drift invalidates it
trust_score: 0.55                # blended; gates survival at floor (ExpeL), not decorative
---

## Claim
For T (e.g. "large-file refactor"), **A > B**.

## Evidence (the earned part — append-only outcome log)
- 2026-06-02 · predicted: refactor drops file <300 lines · observed: passed · method A · HIT
- 2026-06-14 · tried B · user reverted to A next turn · REVERSAL (strongest oracle-free signal)
- 2026-06-28 · A recalled, no failure-signal fired · WEAK-POSITIVE (silent success — nudge only)

## Why (rationale, single-trajectory opinion — labeled as such)
A avoids the shell-escaping pitfall B hits (source: one observed trajectory, NOT measured across A/B).

## Cycle / contradiction flag
none    # if C>A appears while A>B & B>C hold → set status: contested, surface to user, do NOT auto-pick
```

Key shape decisions, each earned from a lens: (1) PAIRWISE + BASELINE, never absolute (DPO reference-anchor). (2) An append-only EVIDENCE LOG of prediction→observation discrepancies, not a single verdict (deliberate-practice / N-of-1 replication). (3) status + n_episodes + confounds make the confidence VISIBLE — the file cannot assert more than it has seen (N1OS "provisional" honesty). (4) REVERSAL and prediction-MISS entries are strong; silent-success entries are weak (prune-asymmetry baked into the schema). (5) decays_after + a cycle flag route contradictions to the human instead of faking a winner. This is a natural extension of cmk's existing fact file + trust_score + DECISIONS.md journal — the judgment file is the DECISIONS.md entry given an evidence log and a confidence stamp.
transferable_kernels: PROPOSE ORACLE-FREE, ADJUDICATE ONLY IF YOU CAN, OTHERWISE STORE AS PROVISIONAL (the GEPA/SkillAdaptor split, downgraded). The reflection-writes-a-candidate step needs NO oracle — an LLM reading a failed session can propose 'method A would have beaten B here, because X'. cmk can ALWAYS do this. What it usually cannot do is the adjudication (re-run + score). Kernel: mint the proposal, tag it provisional, and let cmk's EXISTING disinterested signals (user-correction, cmk-forget, tool-result, re-ask, contradiction) be the only thing that can PROMOTE it to corroborated or PRUNE it. Precondition/limit: promotion is slow and mostly one-directional (you'll prune far more than you promote); a proposal may sit provisional forever if the situation never recurs — that is honest, not a failure.

PRE-REGISTER THE EXPECTATION, STORE THE PREDICTION-ERROR, NOT THE VERDICT (the cognitive-science kernel — the highest-leverage single addition). Before the agent commits to a method, record a one-line EXPECTED outcome ('this should make the test pass' / 'I expect the user to accept this edit'). Next turn, cmk's automatic signals resolve it hit/miss, and the memory written is the DISCREPANCY. This converts a bare fact into an earned judgment with NO oracle and NO human grade, because the reference was self-set and the observation is automatic. Precondition/limit: NO pre-registered expectation → NO better-than claim allowed (the AAR 'intent-first' rule); a self-graded vague expectation always looks 'met' (illusion-of-competence) — so the expectation must be specific and checkable, and only prediction-MISSES are trustworthy (met predictions nudge, never lock).

REVERSAL (A/B/A) IS THE SINGLE STRONGEST ORACLE-FREE SIGNAL A LONE USER EMITS (N-of-1 + delayed-dueling). When the user tries A, switches to B, then reverts to A and stays — the effect reversed with the treatment, within-subject, no oracle. This is a genuine causal signal a single non-replayable stream CAN produce, and it maps onto cmk's dead-end-veto / rejected-edit-buffer signals. Precondition/limit: reversals are rare and you must not confound them with context-drift (was it really the method, or a changed codebase?); flag the confound rather than assume causation.

CYCLE = CONTRADICTION-FLAG, NOT A NOISE-DELETE (the honest INVERSION of TCR at tiny-n). At scale, transitivity denoising deletes cycle edges as measurement error. At n≈2 per pair, a cycle (A>B, B>C, C>A) is more likely GENUINE multi-criteria intransitivity (A faster, B simpler, C portable — no total order exists) than noise. Kernel: reuse cmk's EXISTING fact-contradiction machinery on method-preferences — when a cycle appears, DON'T pick a winner; surface 'these methods are context-dependent, which criterion matters?' to the user. Detecting 'there is no single better' IS a valuable earned judgment. Precondition/limit: only works because cmk already computes contradictions; it's a detector, not a resolver — it hands the fork to the human.

CONFIDENCE-GATE PROMOTION; STORE n AND 'PROVISIONAL' ON EVERY JUDGMENT (the bandit + N-of-1 kernel — makes the scale-floor a FEATURE). Never surface 'A>B' as settled from few episodes; store it with its replication count and only promote past a threshold with a CONSISTENT direction. Below threshold, tag low-confidence explicitly ('I've only seen this twice'). Precondition/limit: this doesn't earn confidence the data lacks — it PREVENTS the lie of unearned confidence, which is the realistic single-user win. Most judgments stay provisional; that is the correct state.

MAKE THE UTILITY GATE SURVIVAL, NOT JUST RANK (ExpeL's count→prune-at-0; already named in ADR-0017(c)). A judgment whose evidence log accumulates failures should become a PRUNE candidate, not an inert low number — the fix for the field-wide 'inert socket' anti-pattern (letta/MemOS/A-Mem/cmk's own decorative trust_score). Precondition/limit: pruning is the reliable direction (prune-asymmetry); do NOT symmetrically 'promote at ceiling' on silent successes — those are weak and self-confirming.

WINDOWED DECAY — A JUDGMENT EXPIRES (sliding-window UCB / non-stationary bandits). An 'A>B' earned six months and one tooling-pivot ago is stale; attach a recency window and let a user-correction that reverses a prior verdict act as a change-point that resets it. Precondition/limit: for a single user the window short enough to track drift is too short to be statistically powerful — the two constraints don't overlap, so decay is an honesty mechanism (don't over-trust old verdicts), not a precision mechanism.
synthesis.the_scale_tension:
YES — earning a VERIFIED "A>B" structurally requires more episodes/comparisons than a single-user IDE kit gets, and this is the load-bearing tension, confirmed independently by four lenses:

- Bandit best-arm-ID: ~Δ⁻²·ln(1/δ) pulls PER ARM — hundreds for a modest gap, thousands for a subtle one.
- Bradley-Terry/TCR: n≥4-8 overlapping comparisons per item for stable strength estimates; at n=3 the denoiser cannot separate real cycles from noise (TCR's own named failure regime).
- Deliberate practice: Ericsson-grade earned judgment needs YEARS of goal-referenced repetition with a coach shaping the goal — cmk has no coach and few repeats.
- N-of-1: precision ≈ σ²/(t·α(1−α)) — grows only with more A/B periods; the within-subject window short enough to track a single dev's drift is too short to be powerful.

A single user emits dozens of durable method-verdicts per YEAR, each near-unique in context, single-arm, non-replayable. That is squarely below every one of these floors. So a single episode/session genuinely CANNOT earn verified comparative judgment — full stop.

AND THIS LOOPS BACK EXACTLY TO THE AGENT-HOST / MULTI-EPISODE DIRECTION (ADR-0017's SRE-agent insight, and Task 50/127). ADR-0017 already reframes: "a session is a bounded agent run," "the kit is the cross-session runtime," "the difference between hosts is episode length + how automated the verdict is, not kind." The scale tension is the QUANTITATIVE case for that reframe: the denominator that matters is not per-session but PER-PROJECT-LIFETIME across all episodes — and a MORE AGENTIC host (an SRE agent: alert→act→log→next alert, 24/7) supplies BOTH (a) more episodes per unit time AND (b) a richer, more automated judge (a monitor/tool-result closer to an oracle). So the same architecture that closes the failure-loop also RAISES the episode count and the judge quality — the two things the scale floor demands. Honest implication: cmk-in-Claude-Code will earn method-judgments SLOWLY and mostly provisionally (few episodes, weak judge); cmk-as-glue-for-an-agent-host is where the loop actually reaches statistical traction. The comparative-judgment feature is a REASON to pursue the agent-host direction, not a thing to expect from the IDE alone. This is precisely the "judge is the per-host adapter" spectrum — the richer the host, the more the A>B machinery becomes affordable.
synthesis.next_move:
HONEST recommendation: cmk is READY for exactly ONE oracle-free wedge now — the propose-provisional + prediction-error kernel — and NOT ready for anything that claims verified A>B. In priority order:

1. TASK (ready now, small, high-leverage): "Provisional method-judgment record + expectation pre-registration." Add the `type: judgment` markdown file (the shape above) and a lightweight pre-action expectation note that the EXISTING passive signals resolve hit/miss. This ships an EARNED-judgment shape without needing a new oracle, and it's a natural extension of DECISIONS.md + trust_score. Done-criterion (per the automatic-path rule): a judgment gets proposed + its evidence-log gets an entry with NO manual `cmk` command — the Stop hook / auto-extract does it. This is the ONLY move that's genuinely ready.

2. ADR LINE (append to ADR-0017, do not open a new ADR): record the finding that earning COMPARATIVE judgment (A>B) is a strictly harder, mostly-unsolved-oracle-free problem DISTINCT from the failure-signal portfolio ADR-0017 already covers — and that the honest design consequence is: cmk stores method-judgments as PROVISIONAL, human-flaggable proposals; it PRUNES on disinterested failure signals but PROMOTES only slowly and never auto-commits a model-self-preference to a hard ranking. This is new evidence extending §20.3's blended-rank position: the blend must gate on CONFIDENCE (n_episodes + direction-consistency), not just similarity ⊕ trust. One paragraph, one decision-log entry.

3. NOT READY — name the prerequisite honestly: a VERIFIED A>B ranking that changes retrieval order is NOT ready, because (a) the scale floor isn't met in the IDE host and (b) the self-judge circularity has no independent rater. The prerequisite is the AGENT-HOST direction (Task 50/127): more episodes + a more automated judge. Do NOT build a "rank by learned method-preference" feature for Claude Code and claim it's earned — it would be a small-sample point estimate dressed as a judgment, exactly the cheat the maintainer is guarding against.

4. DEEPER STUDY (deferred, named trigger per D-248): "same-task replay for cmk" — the ONE thing that would unlock ReasoningBank-strong self-contrast oracle-free. Trigger: when a host can re-attempt a task (an agent loop with a resettable action, not a human conversation). Until then, self-contrast is out of reach and this stays parked.
synthesis.honesty_note: Where I am confident vs not: I am CONFIDENT in the cohort verdict (zero of the 10 systems earn a verified A>B oracle-free from a single non-replayable trajectory — the evidence is explicit and I quote-checked the load-bearing ones against the provided verbatim quotes; e.g. AlphaOPT's "a task is considered successful if the LLM-generated optimal value closely aligns with the provided ground-truth solution" and MUSE's own deferral of contrastive learning to future work). I did NOT re-fetch or re-read the primary papers — I trust the provided verbatim quotes and evidence_level tags as given; if any quote was misattributed upstream, my grouping inherits that error. I read cmk's ADR-0017 directly (primary source, in-repo) to ensure my synthesis EXTENDS its settled positions (prune-asymmetry, judge-as-adapter, inert-socket finding) rather than re-litigating them — my 'next_move' deliberately appends to 0017 rather than proposing a rival ADR, per the decision-log discipline. Two places I am deliberately NOT flattering: (a) I do NOT claim the transferable kernels 'solve' oracle-free comparative judgment — every kernel is explicitly capped (propose≠adjudicate; prediction-error only for the prune direction; provisional-not-verified; reversal is rare and confound-prone). (b) I state plainly that the IDE host cannot meet the scale floor and that pursuing verified A>B there would be the exact cheat the maintainer fears.
